description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, evoking a classic mid-20th-century design. The front panel features a large circular display with a colorful map or frequency indicator, flanked by a horizontal metallic strip and a green indicator light. Below the display, there are four black rotary knobs, likely for tuning, volume, and other controls. The left section of the front panel has a textured grille, suggesting the location of the speaker. The sides and back are plain, finished in a rich, dark wood grain, with no visible ports or openings. The overall appearance is robust and decorative, suitable for display in urban interiors, cafes, or retro-themed public spaces.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a circular display, four black knobs, and a textured speaker grille on the front. The body is finished in dark wood with a classic, retro aesthetic.
description_view_0: The front view shows the circular display, four black knobs, a green indicator light, and a textured speaker grille.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, smooth wooden surface with no visible features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat, unadorned wooden panel, emphasizing the boxy shape.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain wooden surface with a slight curve at the front edge.
